KpiObservabilityConfig.description:
KPI sink configuration shared across all Fred backends.

Defaults enable Prometheus + OpenSearch (prod-ready out of the box).
For local dev, disable both and enable log instead:

    observability:
      kpi:
        log:
          enabled: true
        prometheus:
          enabled: false
        opensearch:
          enabled: false

process_metrics_interval_sec.description: Emit process/SQL-pool KPIs every N seconds. 0 to disable.
process_metrics_interval_sec.title: Process Metrics Interval Sec

KpiObservabilityConfig.title: KpiObservabilityConfig

LangfuseObservabilityConfig.description:
Langfuse connection settings.

Only non-secret settings live here.
Credentials go in the .env file as LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY and LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY.
LangfuseObservabilityConfig.title: LangfuseObservabilityConfig

OpenFgaRebacConfig.description: Configuration for an OpenFGA-backed relationship engine.

enabled.description: To disable ReBAC checks (do not disable in production). If OIDC (UserSecurity and M2MSecurity) ReBAC check will be disabled even if this is true.
enabled.title: Enabled

api_url.description: Base URL for the OpenFGA HTTP API (e.g. https://fga.example.com)
api_url.title: Api Url

store_name.description: Name of the OpenFGA store to use
store_name.title: Store Name

authorization_model_id.description: Optional authorization model ID to use for read operations. Will be overridden if sync_schema_on_init is True.
authorization_model_id.title: Authorization Model Id

create_store_if_needed.description: Create the OpenFGA store if it does not already exist
create_store_if_needed.title: Create Store If Needed

sync_schema_on_init.description: Synchronize the authorization model when creating the engine
sync_schema_on_init.title: Sync Schema On Init

token_env_var.description: Environment variable that stores the OpenFGA API token
token_env_var.title: Token Env Var

timeout_millisec.description: Timeout in milliseconds for OpenFGA API requests. Defaults to 5000 so a stalled OpenFGA call fails fast with an error instead of hanging the request indefinitely (set to None only to explicitly disable the timeout).
timeout_millisec.title: Timeout Millisec

headers.description: Static HTTP headers to send with each OpenFGA API request
headers.title: Headers

OpenFgaRebacConfig.title: OpenFgaRebacConfig

PodAIConfig.description:
AI model and knowledge-flow settings for an agent pod.

Why this section exists separately from AgentAppSettings:
- pods only need the outbound Knowledge Flow base URL plus runtime HTTP
  timeout tuning shared by KF and MCP adapters
- gateway-only concerns such as attachment/session limits should not leak
  into the pod config contract

Example:
- `PodAIConfig(knowledge_flow_url="http://localhost:8111/knowledge-flow/v1", timeout=RuntimeTimeouts(connect=20, read=60))`

timeout.description: Timeout settings for pod outbound HTTP calls to Knowledge Flow and other runtime adapters.

PodAIConfig.title: PodAIConfig

PodAppConfig.description:
Basic HTTP server and local observability settings for an agent pod.

Why this exists:
- every pod needs the same HTTP binding knobs plus the small Prometheus/KPI
  settings already used by the other Fred backends
- keeping these fields in `app` preserves the familiar startup contract for
  local benches and scrape-based debugging

How to use it:
- keep the defaults for simple local development
- set `metrics_port` / `metrics_address` when `observability.metrics` is
  `prometheus`

Example:
- `PodAppConfig(base_url="/pod/v1", port=8000, metrics_port=9115)`

limit_concurrency.description: Optional maximum number of concurrent HTTP or WebSocket connections accepted by Uvicorn. Leave unset to disable the limit.
limit_concurrency.title: Limit Concurrency

PodAppConfig.title: PodAppConfig

PodObservabilityConfig.description:
Observability provider selection for a Fred agent pod.

Non-secret settings (backend choice, endpoints) live in configuration.yaml.
Credentials (API keys, tokens) stay in the .env file.

Example:
    observability:
      tracer: logging       # null | logging | langfuse
      kpi:
        log:
          enabled: true
        prometheus:
          enabled: false
        opensearch:
          enabled: false
      langfuse:
        host: "http://localhost:3001"
        # LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY and LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY in .env
PodObservabilityConfig.title: PodObservabilityConfig

PodPlatformConfig.description:
Small set of Fred platform service URLs needed by the pod runtime.

Why this exists:
- agent pods stay execution-focused, but some execution paths still need a
  central Fred service such as control-plane for managed agent-instance
  resolution

How to use it:
- set `control_plane_url` when the pod should accept `agent_instance_id`
  execution requests (the pod resolves the instance's template+tuning from
  the control-plane team-scoped binding)

Example:
- `PodPlatformConfig(control_plane_url="http://localhost:8222/control-plane/v1")`
PodPlatformConfig.title: PodPlatformConfig

PodSchedulerConfig.description:
Temporal scheduler settings for an agent pod.

Disabled by default — only needed for Story B (deep/durable agents).
Reuses TemporalSchedulerConfig from fred-core.
PodSchedulerConfig.title: PodSchedulerConfig

PodStorageConfig.description:
Persistence backend settings for an agent pod.

All conversation state (sessions, multi-turn history, checkpoints) is
managed exclusively by the LangGraph SQL checkpointer, which uses the
`postgres` connection. There are no separate session or history tables.

Fields:
- `postgres`: SQL engine used by the LangGraph checkpointer (SQLite in
  local dev via sqlite_path, PostgreSQL in production via host/port/database)
- `opensearch`: optional, for log forwarding in production
- `log_store`: optional, for structured log persistence
PodStorageConfig.title: PodStorageConfig

sqlite_path.description: Path to the SQLite database file (for local dev/testing).
sqlite_path.title: Sqlite Path

pool_size.description: Optional pool size for the engine.
pool_size.title: Pool Size

max_overflow.description: Optional max_overflow for SQLAlchemy pool (defaults to SQLAlchemy's 10 if unset).
max_overflow.title: Max Overflow

pool_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for a connection from the pool before timing out.
pool_timeout.title: Pool Timeout

pool_recycle.description: Recycle connections after this many seconds (prevents stale TCP / server timeouts).
pool_recycle.title: Pool Recycle

pool_pre_ping.description: Enable SQLAlchemy pool_pre_ping to evict stale connections.
pool_pre_ping.title: Pool Pre Ping

RuntimeTimeouts.description:
Minimal timeout settings for outbound runtime HTTP clients.

Why this exists:
- KF/MCP clients need consistent timeout settings across runtimes

How to use it:
- construct with explicit connect/read values
- call `as_httpx_timeout_config()` to feed httpx
RuntimeTimeouts.title: RuntimeTimeouts

SchedulerBackend.description:
Allowed scheduler backend values across Fred backends.

Why this exists:
- Avoid raw string literals (`"temporal"`, `"memory"`) spread in business code.
- Keep one typed source of truth used by configuration parsing and runtime checks.

How to use:
```python
from fred_core.scheduler import SchedulerBackend

if backend == SchedulerBackend.MEMORY:
    ...
```
SchedulerBackend.title: SchedulerBackend

profile.description: Hardened security profile (RUNTIME-07). 'c3' forces strict JWT issuer/audience validation, forbids no-security/mock-admin, and requires OpenFGA ReBAC enabled (pod-side authorization, fail-closed) — failing startup otherwise. The control-plane issues no signed grant.
profile.title: Profile

rpc_timeout_seconds.description: Deadline applied to individual Temporal RPC calls (start_workflow, describe) so a stuck Temporal frontend cannot hang the caller indefinitely.
rpc_timeout_seconds.title: Rpc Timeout Seconds

ingestion_workflow_parallelism.description: Max number of files launched in parallel per parent ingestion workflow.
ingestion_workflow_parallelism.title: Ingestion Workflow Parallelism

ingestion_max_concurrent_workflow_tasks.description: Max concurrent Temporal workflow tasks processed by a worker process.
ingestion_max_concurrent_workflow_tasks.title: Ingestion Max Concurrent Workflow Tasks

ingestion_max_concurrent_activities.description: Max concurrent Temporal activities processed by a worker process.
ingestion_max_concurrent_activities.title: Ingestion Max Concurrent Activities

TracerBackend.description:
Distributed tracing backend for the agent pod.

- null     — no tracing, all spans are dropped
- logging  — each span is emitted as a structured log entry (default)
- langfuse — spans are sent to a Langfuse server;
             credentials (LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY) must be
             set in the .env file; host is configured in the langfuse section
TracerBackend.title: TracerBackend

description:
Complete structured configuration for a Fred agent pod.

Mirrors agentic-backend's `Configuration` model, scoped to what an agent
pod actually needs. All field types are reused from fred-core and fred-sdk
— no new types invented here.

How to use it:
- write a `config/configuration.yaml` alongside `config/models_catalog.yaml`
  and `config/mcp_catalog.yaml`
- call `load_agent_pod_config()` at startup
- pass the result to `create_agent_app(config=...)`

The `security` field is a `SecurityConfiguration` from fred-core, the same
type used in agentic-backend. When `security.user.enabled` is True,
`create_agent_app` enforces Keycloak JWT validation on every endpoint.
title: AgentPodConfig
